claims.claim: Pipecat SegmentedSTTService buffers audio in a bytearray and on VADUserStoppedSpeakingFrame wraps the buffer in WAV (setframerate(self.sample_rate)) and makes one run_stt() call
claims.found: Line 762 is `_handle_user_started_speaking` (just sets _user_speaking=True). The WAV-wrapping + run_stt actually happen in `_handle_user_stopped_speaking` lines 765-780: `wav.setframerate(self.sample_rate)` (772), `wav.writeframes(self._audio_buffer)` (773), `await self.process_generator(self.run_stt(content.read()))` (780). bytearray buffer at line 726.
claims.correction: stt_service.py:765-780 (the run_stt-on-stop logic); buffer bytearray at :726

claims.claim: Every emitted TranscriptionFrame is force-finalized=True with docstring 'Segmented STT services process complete speech segments and return a single TranscriptionFrame per segment'
claims.found: Lines 742-743 docstring: 'Segmented STT services process complete speech segments and return a single TranscriptionFrame per segment, so every transcription is inherently finalized.' Lines 749-750: `if isinstance(frame, TranscriptionFrame): frame.finalized = True`.

claims.claim: Keeps a ~1s pre-roll buffer (_audio_buffer_size_1s = sample_rate*2) to recover audio before VAD fired
claims.found: Line 737: `self._audio_buffer_size_1s = self.sample_rate * 2`. Lines 804-807 maintain the small buffer when not speaking. sample_rate*2 = 1s of 16-bit (2-byte) mono audio = correct.

claims.claim: LiveKit StreamAdapter forwards frames to VAD, and on END_OF_SPEECH merges frames and calls recognize() once -> emits one FINAL_TRANSCRIPT
claims.found: Lines 110-139: `_recognize()` iterates vad_stream; on `VADEventType.END_OF_SPEECH` (115) it does `merged_frames = utils.merge_frames(event.frames)` (122), `await self._wrapped_stt.recognize(buffer=merged_frames, ...)` (123), then sends `SpeechEventType.FINAL_TRANSCRIPT` (134-138). Forwarding to vad via `_forward_input` (100-106).

claims.claim: Modal: VAD+turn-detection to segment audio passed to Parakeet was FASTER than open-weights streaming STT they tried; no partial real-time feel
claims.found: Line 33: 'using Pipecat's local VAD + turn detection to segment audio and passing that to Parakeet was FASTER than the open-weights streaming STT implementations they tried. No partial-transcript real-time feel'.

claims.claim: Quote: 'the only thing that matters for total voice-to-voice latency is the final transcript time'
claims.found: Line 33: '"the only thing that matters for total voice-to-voice latency is the final transcript time"'. Line 55 (Modal takeaway) restates: 'Final-transcript time is what matters for v2v latency — segment-then-transcribe (VAD-gated) can beat streaming STT on total latency.' Note quotes 'final-transcript time'; source has 'final transcript time' (no hyphen) — immaterial.

claims.claim: STT is a separate Parakeet WebSocket service that receives a segment, not a continuous partial stream
claims.found: Lines 30-38 'Modal architecture — TRANSPORT MAP': line 35 'STT Service `nvidia/parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v3` over WebSocket + modal.Tunnel'; line 38 'STT and TTS use persistent WebSockets'. The diagram shows STT as separate Parakeet WebSocket service. The 'receives a segment, not a partial stream' framing is an inference consistent with modal-v2v:33 (segment-then-transcribe), not literally stated in the diagram, but well-supported by the combined sources.

claims.claim: Frontmatter code_ref pipecat/src/pipecat/services/stt_service.py:762 points to SegmentedSTTService mechanism
claims.found: Class `SegmentedSTTService(STTService)` begins at line 704. Line 762 is `_handle_user_started_speaking`. The core mechanism (stop-handler run_stt) is 765-780; class def is 704.

claims.claim: Frontmatter code_ref stream_adapter.py:110 points to merge+recognize-on-END_OF_SPEECH
claims.found: Line 110 is `async def _recognize()`, which contains the END_OF_SPEECH merge_frames + recognize + FINAL_TRANSCRIPT logic (112-139). Correct anchor.

summary: All mechanism and source-figure claims are accurate and well-supported by ground truth. Two MISCITED code anchors: the inline citation 'stt_service.py:762-780' and frontmatter ':762' point to `_handle_user_started_speaking`, while the buffer-WAV-run_stt mechanism the note describes lives in `_handle_user_stopped_speaking` (765-780); the class itself starts at 704. No WRONG claims. The modal-v2v quotes and diagram transport map are verbatim/consistent. Overall PARTIAL due to off-by-a-few-line code anchors.
